name: Scales of Maat
description: The Scales of Maat are a central artifact in Egyptian mythology, representing cosmic balance, truth, and justice. These scales are used in the Hall of Two Truths during the Weighing of the Heart ceremony in the afterlife. Anubis, the jackal-headed god of embalming and the dead, oversees the weighing. On one side of the scale is placed the heart of the deceased, representing their life and actions. On the other side is placed the feather of Maat, representing truth, justice, and cosmic order. If the heart is lighter than or equal to the feather, the deceased is deemed worthy and allowed to pass into the afterlife to be with Osiris. However, if the heart is heavier than the feather, weighed down by sin and evil deeds, it is devoured by Ammit, the Devourer of Souls, a creature with the head of a crocodile, the forequarters of a lion, and the hindquarters of a hippopotamus, resulting in eternal oblivion. The Scales of Maat are not merely a physical object but a powerful symbol of moral responsibility and the consequences of one's actions in life. They underscore the importance of living a life aligned with the principles of Maat, emphasizing truth, order, and righteousness. The concept embodies the Egyptian belief in a just and ordered universe, where actions have consequences and the soul is ultimately judged based on its adherence to these principles.
subtitle: Balance, Truth, and Justice in Afterlife
origin: Created by the gods to maintain cosmic order
currentLocation: Hall of Two Truths in the Duat (Underworld)

keyMyths.title: The Weighing of the Heart
keyMyths.description: The Scales of Maat are central to the Weighing of the Heart ceremony. If the heart is heavier than the feather of Maat, the deceased is condemned.

keyMyths.title: Maat's Role
keyMyths.description: Maat, the goddess of truth and justice, is symbolized by the feather placed on the scales. Her presence ensures the fairness of the judgment.

sources.author: E.A. Wallis Budge
sources.work: The Egyptian Book of the Dead
sources.description: Translation and analysis of the Book of the Dead, which details the Weighing of the Heart ceremony.
sources.text: Translation and analysis of the Book of the Dead, which details the Weighing of the Heart ceremony.
